---
title: "2.3 if Statements"
subtitle: "Part 3: Magic 8 Ball Challenge"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit2-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Groupwork Coding Challenge

### Magic 8 Ball

![Magic 8 Ball](../../assets/2-selection-and-iteration/2-3-if-statements/Magic_eight_ball.png){fig-align="center" width="18%"}

Have you ever seen a Magic 8 ball? You ask it a yes-no question and then shake it to get a random response like `Signs point to yes!`, `Very doubtful`, etc.

If you've never seen a Magic 8 ball, check out this [simulator](https://magic-8ball.com/).

## Challenge Context

### Random responses from 1 to 8

In the exercise below, come up with <span class="mark">8 responses</span> to yes-no questions.

Write a program that chooses a random number from `1` to `8` and then uses if statements to test the number and print out the associated random response from `1-8`.

If you need help with random numbers, remember the formula:

```java
(int) (Math.random() * max) + min
```

## Code Task

### `activecode:: challenge-Magic8ball`

Textbook prompt: Complete the `printRandomResponse()` method to print out 1 of 8 random responses and the `lucky()` method to toss a coin and print out `"Lucky!"` or `"No Luck!"` based on the result.

Run the code multiple times to see the responses.

## Code Window

### `activecode:: challenge-Magic8ball`

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
public class Magic8Ball
{
    public static void printRandomResponse()
    {
        // 1. Get a random number from 1 to 8

        // 2. Use if statements to test the random number
        //    and print out 1 of 8 random responses

     }

     public static void lucky()
     {
         // 3. Use Math.random() to toss a coin to choose between 2 choices
         // 4. Use an if/else statement to test the random number
         //    and print out "Lucky!" or else "No Luck!"

     }

     public static void main(String[] args)
     {
         String question = "Will it rain tomorrow?";
         System.out.println(question);
         printRandomResponse();
         lucky();
     }
}
```
:::

## Expected Behavior

### `challenge-Magic8ball`

The completed program should:

::: {.tight-list}
- print the question from `main`
- print 1 of 8 random Magic 8 Ball responses
- print either `Lucky!` or `No Luck!`
- show different responses when run multiple times
:::

## Test Requirements

### Runestone tests

Runestone checks that:

::: {.tight-list}
- `main` prints a statement with output length greater than `0`
- running `main` 200 times produces at least `8` unique outputs
- the code has at least `8` if statements
- the `lucky()` method contains an `else` statement
:::

## Student Deliverable

### Required steps

::: {.tight-list}
- choose a random integer from `1` to `8`
- write 8 if statements that connect the random number to 8 different responses
- use `Math.random()` again to choose between 2 lucky outcomes
- use an if/else statement to print `"Lucky!"` or `"No Luck!"`
- run multiple times and check that responses vary
:::

## Optional Extension

### User input version

The textbook notes that you can make this code more interactive by using the `Scanner` class to have the user ask a question first.

Try your code with input in JuiceMind, replit, or a local IDE. This extension is optional for classroom completion.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- keep the `Magic8Ball` class structure and method names
- use `(int) (Math.random() * max) + min` to produce a bounded random integer
- connect random values `1` through `8` to 8 different printed responses
- use separate if statements for the 8 response cases
- use if/else in `lucky()` for the two lucky outcomes
- test repeated runs instead of judging from one output
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
